content:
Key Findings

  • Hyndburn Borough Council's spending increased by £2.2M (29.7%) from 2017 to 2026
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £71.7M (1587.9%) from 2023 to 2024
  • The largest year-over-year decrease was £5,249,992 (53.8%) from 2022 to 2023
  • Spending peaked at £76.2M in 2024 before dropping to £9.5M in 2026

Year-by-Year Spending Trends

Hyndburn Borough Council's spending has fluctuated significantly over the past decade. In 2017, the council spent £7.3M, which increased to £9.2M in 2018, a rise of £1.9M or 26.2%. However, this was followed by a notable decrease to £6.4M in 2019, a drop of £2,834,079 or 30.7%. The spending then rose again to £8.0M in 2020, marking an increase of £1.6M or 25.3%.

The following years saw more fluctuations, with spending reaching £9.5M in 2021, £9.8M in 2022, and then a significant drop to £4.5M in 2023. The most dramatic change occurred between 2023 and 2024, where spending surged by £71.7M, a staggering increase of 1587.9%, reaching £76.2M. This peak was followed by a decrease to £70.9M in 2025 and a further drop to £9.5M in 2026.

Analysing the Peaks and Troughs

The data reveals that Hyndburn Borough Council's spending has experienced significant volatility. The largest year-over-year increase was from 2023 to 2024, where spending jumped from £4.5M to £76.2M. This substantial rise could be attributed to various factors, such as increased investment in infrastructure, emergency spending, or other significant financial commitments. Conversely, the largest decrease occurred from 2022 to 2023, where spending dropped by £5,249,992 or 53.8%, from £9.8M to £4.5M.

Understanding these fluctuations requires further context, which could be obtained through Freedom of Information requests or by examining the council's published contracts register. The significant changes in spending patterns highlight the importance of transparency and accountability in public spending.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Hyndburn Borough Council about the reasons behind the significant spending increases and decreases in specific years.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to gain insights into the council's financial decisions and spending priorities.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to understand where the funds are being allocated and to whom.
  • Write to your local councillor about the fluctuations in spending and request more detailed explanations for the significant changes observed in the data.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and conduct your own analysis of Hyndburn Borough Council's financial transactions.
This article is based on Hyndburn Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
